info.title: Staff Scheduler API
info.description:
Open-source workforce management REST API.

All non-public endpoints require a JWT obtained via `POST /api/v1/auth/login`, presented either as the httpOnly `token` cookie (set automatically at login) or as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

Base URLs:
- Canonical: `http://localhost:3001/api/v1`
- Legacy (will be removed): `http://localhost:3001/api`

Every response follows the envelope format:
- Success: `{ "success": true, "data": <T> }`
- Error:   `{ "success": false, "error": { "code": "ERROR_CODE", "message": "..." } }`

Validation errors return `VALIDATION_ERROR` with a `details` array of field-level messages.
Rate-limit errors return HTTP 429 with code `RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED`.

bearerAuth.description: JWT passed as Authorization: Bearer <token>. Alternative to the httpOnly cookie for non-browser clients.

cookieAuth.name: token
cookieAuth.description: httpOnly JWT cookie set by POST /auth/login (primary mechanism for the web frontend).

code.description: Machine-readable error code, e.g. VALIDATION_ERROR, NOT_FOUND, UNAUTHORIZED.

role.description: Legacy role field. Use RBAC permissions for fine-grained access control.

orgUnitId.description: Parent org-unit FK. Null if the department is not linked to an org unit.

permissions.description: Permission keys assigned to this role, e.g. 'schedule.manage'.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated but lacking the required permission.

ValidationError.description: Request body or path parameter failed Zod validation.

tags.name: Employees
tags.description: Employee profiles, skills, and department membership.

tags.name: Schedules
tags.description: Schedule lifecycle: create, publish, archive, optimize.

tags.name: Shifts
tags.description: Individual shifts within a schedule, plus shift templates.

tags.name: Assignments
tags.description: Assign employees to shifts, confirm, decline, complete.

tags.name: Calendar
tags.description: iCalendar feeds for personal and department schedules.

tags.name: Directory
tags.description: Employee directory, custom fields, and vCard export.

tags.name: Audit Logs
tags.description: Immutable audit trail for all sensitive actions.

tags.name: Org Units
tags.description: Hierarchical organisational unit tree and employee loans.

tags.name: Skill Gap
tags.description: Skill gap analysis and coverage reports.

post.description: Defines a multi-step approval chain for a specific change type.
Escalates pending approval steps past their deadline.
Assigns an employee to a shift. Validates: capacity, conflicts, availability, skills, compliance limits, and active policies. Requires `assignment.manage`.

400.description: Capacity exceeded, conflict, unavailability, or missing skills.

post.description: Creates multiple assignments in one request. Failures are logged and skipped; the response contains only successfully created assignments. Requires `assignment.manage`.

get.description: Returns users in the shift's department who have no conflicting assignment.
Manager or admin only. Immutable log of all sensitive actions.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: 1-based page number; use together with pageSize for the paginated envelope.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Page size; use together with page for the paginated envelope.

get.description: Requires `audit.read`. Returns all matching entries without row limit (same filters as the list endpoint, no `limit`/`offset`). Use `fromDate`/`toDate` to scope exports.

200.description: Matching audit entries, as JSON envelope or CSV attachment.

get.summary: Get a single audit log entry
get.description: Module `audit` must be enabled; requires `audit.read`.

post.description: Requires a valid current TOTP code or an unused recovery code; answers 401 `TOTP_INVALID` otherwise.
Verifies the 6-digit TOTP code and enables 2FA for the current user. Returns one-time recovery codes — store them safely.

400.description: Invalid code or 2FA not in setup state.

post.description: Generates a TOTP secret and returns it plus an `otpauth://` URI. The caller must confirm the code via `/auth/2fa/enable` before 2FA is active.

Authenticate with email and password. On success the signed JWT (valid for the period configured in `JWT_EXPIRES_IN`, default 24 h) is set as an httpOnly `token` cookie; it is never returned in the response body. Subsequent requests authenticate via that cookie, or alternatively via `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. When the account has two-factor authentication enabled, `totpCode` (a TOTP or single-use recovery code) is required: the endpoint answers 401 `TOTP_REQUIRED` when it is missing and 401 `TOTP_INVALID` when it is wrong.

200.description: Authenticated. The JWT is delivered as an httpOnly `token` cookie.

Set-Cookie.description: httpOnly JWT cookie, e.g. `token=<jwt>; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax`.

401.description: Invalid credentials (`LOGIN_FAILED`), missing 2FA code (`TOTP_REQUIRED`), or wrong 2FA code (`TOTP_INVALID`).

429.description: Login throttled — too many failed attempts.

post.description: Informational endpoint. With JWT-based auth, logout is primarily client-side: the client drops the token from storage. The server responds with success without invalidating the token server-side (no revocation store). See security backlog item B001 for token blacklisting.
Issues a new JWT for an already-authenticated user. The caller must hold a currently-valid token; this endpoint does not accept expired tokens.

get.description: Validates the incoming JWT and returns the user record (without secrets). Use this to check whether a stored token is still valid.
Aggregated iCal for an entire department. Manager/admin only. Pass the manager's calendar token.

403.description: Caller is not a manager of this department.

get.description: Returns an iCal feed for the user identified by `token`. No JWT required — pass the token as a query parameter. Emits ETag for conditional requests. Lists colleagues working the same shift in DESCRIPTION.

post.summary: Get or create calendar feed token
post.description: Returns a stable token used to authenticate the iCalendar feed URL.

post.summary: Rotate the personal calendar feed token
post.description: Invalidates the previous token.

get.description: Returns paginated change requests. Requires change_request.review permission.

post.description: Creates a new change request proposal. Requires change_request.create permission.

get.description: Returns a single change request. Accessible by the proposer or any user with change_request.review.

post.description: Marks the approved request as applied. Audit log attributes the action to the approver (authority holder). Requires change_request.review.
Moves the request from pending to approved. Requires change_request.review.
Cancels a pending request. Accessible by the proposer or any user with change_request.review.
Moves the request to rejected. Requires change_request.review.

get.description: Last audit-log entries. Guarded exactly like /audit-logs: requires the `audit` module enabled for the caller's organization and the `audit.read` permission.

200.description: Stats (total staff, shifts today, coverage, monthly hours/cost, etc.). `monthlyCost` is null without `report.read`.

get.description: Aggregate counters for the landing dashboard. `monthlyCost` (labor cost derived from hourly rates) is only computed for callers holding `report.read`; for other users it is `null`.
Returns delegations involving the caller (as delegator or delegate).

post.description: Grants a set of permissions to another user for a specified period.
Admin only. Existing emails are skipped.

get.description: Returns the caller's profile plus any custom fields.

put.description: Replaces all custom fields for a user. Manager only.

post.description: Creates the employee record for an existing user. Requires `employee.manage`.

delete.summary: Remove a skill from an employee

get.description: Long-lived `text/event-stream` connection delivering domain events.
Returns database connectivity status, service version and timestamp. No authentication required; process metrics are intentionally not exposed.

post.description: Admin only. Idempotent by email — existing employees are updated.
Admin/manager only.

get.summary: List feature modules and their status

put.summary: Enable or disable a feature module

get.summary: List feature modules with per-org overrides applied
get.description: Requires `settings.manage`.

200.description: Module list with org-specific overrides applied.

put.summary: Create or update a per-org module override

delete.summary: Remove a per-org module override, reverting to the global default

get.description: Module `notifications` must be enabled (404 otherwise).

patch.summary: Mark all own notifications as read

get.description: Module `notifications` must be enabled (404 otherwise).

post.description: Requires manager role.

delete.summary: Remove a user from an on-call period

get.summary: List assignments of an on-call period

get.description: Employee loans temporarily move a user to a different org unit.

post.description: Requires `loan.request`.
Requires `org_unit.manage`.

get.description: Returns the full hierarchy as a nested tree structure.

get.summary: List members of an org unit

post.summary: Add a member to an org unit
post.description: Requires `employee.manage`.

get.summary: List members of an org unit with display details
get.description: Same membership as GET /org/units/{id}/members, enriched with each member's name, email, and position — used by the org-chart view and the structure-decision delegate picker.

get.summary: Chain of superiors for the caller
get.description: Same as GET /org/manager-chain/{userId}, defaulted to the authenticated caller.

get.summary: Chain of superiors for a user
get.description: Walks up from the target user's primary org unit to the root, resolving each unit's manager. Open to any authenticated user, same visibility as the rest of the org tree.

delete.summary: Remove a member from an org unit

patch.summary: Set a member's primary org unit

post.description: Requires `policy.manage`.

post.summary: Cancel an own policy exception request

post.description: Dry-runs policy checks without creating an assignment. Returns violations.

get.description: Measures how evenly shifts are distributed across employees in a schedule.
Returns the responsibility matrix. Requires responsibility.read.

post.description: Adds a new entry to the responsibility matrix. Requires responsibility.manage.

get.description: Returns the user IDs of everyone responsible for a given permission over a specific subject. Requires responsibility.read. departmentIds and roleIds are comma-separated integers (max 100 each).

delete.description: Removes a rule from the responsibility matrix. Requires responsibility.manage.

post.description: Requires admin.

get.description: Requires `role.manage`.

post.summary: Grant a role to a user
post.description: Requires `role.manage`.

delete.summary: Revoke a role from a user

post.description: Requires `schedule.manage`. Creates a draft schedule. A schedule cannot overlap with an existing draft or published schedule in the same department.

409.description: Overlapping schedule exists for this department and date range.

put.description: Updates name, dates, notes, or status. Requires `schedule.manage`. Archived schedules cannot be modified.

delete.description: Only draft schedules can be deleted. Cascades to shifts and assignments. Requires `schedule.manage`.

400.description: Schedule is not in draft status.

post.description: Clones the schedule and all its shifts to a new date range. Requires `schedule.manage`.
Runs the scheduling optimizer (OR-Tools if available, TypeScript fallback otherwise) and creates pending assignments for all open shifts. Requires `schedule.optimize`.

patch.description: Transitions status from `draft` to `published`. The schedule must have at least one shift. Requires `schedule.publish`.

get.description: Full settings catalog. Requires the `settings.manage` permission; regular users read /settings/currency and /settings/time-period instead.

post.summary: Reset a setting to its default
post.description: Requires `settings.manage`.

post.summary: Cancel an own shift swap request

post.description: Requires `shift.manage`.
Requires `shift.manage`.

200.description: Gaps between required and available skills.

get.description: Returns mode (production | demo | development), version, and active modules. No authentication required.
Employees see their own requests; managers see all requests in their departments.

post.description: Manager action.

409.description: An open attendance record already exists for this user.

post.summary: Clock out on own open attendance record

409.description: Record is already clocked out or not owned by the caller.

get.summary: List attendance records (own, or all for attendance.read/attendance.approve holders)
Planned vs. actual labor cost for a date range
get.description: Requires `attendance.read` and the `payroll` module.

get.summary: Get one attendance record (own or approver)

post.description: Requires `attendance.approve`.

409.description: Record is not pending or still clocked in.

post.description: Requires `attendance.approve`.

get.description: Returns the user directory. Callers holding `user.read_all` get the complete, unscoped list; everyone else receives only users in departments they manage.

post.description: Creates a new user account. Requires admin permissions.

put.description: Update email, name, role, or active status. Admins can update any user; regular users can update themselves (limited fields).

delete.description: Permanently deletes a user. Admin only.

post.description: Assigns the same role to multiple users in a single request. Requires `role.manage`. Each assignment is individually audit-logged.

assigned.description: Number of users who received the role.

get.description: Returns a matrix of all responsibility rules grouped by entity type and org unit. Requires `responsibility.read`.
Returns the responsibility rules that apply to the authenticated user based on their role and org-unit membership.

post.description: Creates multiple responsibility rules in one request. Requires `responsibility.manage`.

get.summary: Get conflicts for a responsibility rule
get.description: Returns other rules that conflict with (overlap) the given rule. Requires `responsibility.read`.
Returns approval items assigned to the current user. Filter by `status` query param.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by approval status. Omit to return all.

200.description: Paginated list of pending approval items.

get.description: Returns the count of approval items currently in `pending` status for the authenticated user.

post.description: Records an approval decision for the given pending-approval item and advances the workflow step.
Records a rejection decision for the given pending-approval item and closes the workflow step.

post.summary: Structure head keeps a decision for themselves
post.description: For a decision assigned to an org unit as a whole, the unit head decides to keep and decide it personally instead of delegating or opening it to the team. Idempotent — a second call is a no-op if already kept. 403 if the caller is not the unit's head.

200.description: Decision kept by the structure head.

post.summary: Structure head delegates a decision to one team member
post.description: For a decision assigned to an org unit as a whole, the unit head hands it to one specific member of that unit, who becomes the new assignee. 400 if targetUserId is not a member of the structure; 403 if the caller is not the unit's head.

200.description: Decision delegated to the target member.

post.summary: Structure head opens a decision to the whole team
post.description: For a decision assigned to an org unit as a whole, the unit head opens it so that any member of that unit may decide it (not only the head). 403 if the caller is not the unit's head.

get.summary: Chain of command for a decision
get.description: Who this decision was assigned to (structure and head, if applicable), the full history of keep/delegate/open-to-structure actions, and who ultimately decided it. Visible to the original proposer, the current assignee, whoever decided it, and any member of the assigned structure — not just whoever is authorized to decide it.
